scope.summary: Accept Asupersync's exact missing-close-notify EOF only at an empty terminal LOGOFF response boundary, skip the impossible final EOF write after that peer hard-close, and preserve strict pre-ACCEPT, ordinary-read, truncated-response, and normal-close behavior.
in_scope: Terminal session LOGOFF response handling after a usable TLS session
Exact UnexpectedEof classification for the Asupersync missing-close-notify diagnostic
Connection::close tail behavior for modern and classic framing, including no final TNS EOF write after a terminal peer hard-close
out_of_scope: Tolerating missing close_notify during connect, pre-ACCEPT, authentication, or ordinary query responses
Accepting a partial TNS header or payload as a clean close
Changing deterministic TLS configuration failover, which is tracked separately by rust-oracledb-4sfc

entry_points.name: Connection::close terminal wire tail
entry_points.trace: On HEAD (ancestors 3f55d30 + 4eddf9b), `missing_tls_close_notify_is_tolerated_only_at_terminal_session_boundary` drives the production finish_session_close tail used by Connection::close. For modern and classic framing it proves an empty terminal peer hard-close succeeds with no final TNS EOF write; it also proves a fully framed response retains that EOF write. Re-run 2026-07-21: cargo test -p oracledb --lib missing_tls_close_notify_is_tolerated_only_at_terminal_session_boundary → ok. is_missing_tls_close_notify lives at crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs:9444.

entry_points.name: TLS hard-close after complete plaintext
entry_points.trace: `tcps_hard_close_delivers_complete_plaintext_before_missing_close_notify` passes on HEAD (re-run 2026-07-21) against a local rustls server that deliberately closes TCP without writing close_notify.

entry_points.name: TLS hard-close before expected plaintext
entry_points.trace: `tcps_hard_close_before_expected_plaintext_remains_unexpected_eof` passes on HEAD (re-run 2026-07-21) and proves a hard close before the expected plaintext is not normalized into success.

entry_points.name: terminal disposition boundary
entry_points.trace: Commit 3f55d30 confines missing-close-notify normalization to Connection::close's terminal LOGOFF response. Commit 4eddf9b preserves the peer-hard-close disposition through the public close tail so it can skip only the now-impossible EOF write.

limitations: The integration proof uses a local deterministic rustls peer rather than a live OCI endpoint; it reproduces the exact bare-TCP-FIN/no-close_notify transport shape without consuming a cloud resource.
The original required-proof JSON path was not present in this tree; close is pinned by the three tests above re-run green on HEAD with 3f55d30/4eddf9b as ancestors.
